---
title: "owner.analysis"
author: Adriel Evaristo
format: html
---

### Exercise: Explore, Clean, and Wrangle Data

```{r}
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(here)
```

# Read in the data
```{r}
# Read in the data and store the data frame as lobster_abundance
lobster_abundance <- read_csv(here::here("data/Lobster_Abundance_All_Years_20220829.csv"))
```

# Exercises
```{r}
# Exercise 1
lobster_abundance <- lobster_abundance %>% 
    mutate(SIZE_MM = na_if(SIZE_MM, -99999))

# Exercise 2
not_napl <- lobster_abundance %>% 
    filter(SITE != "NAPL")

# Exercise 3
aque_70mm <- lobster_abundance %>% 
    filter(SITE == "AQUE" & SIZE_MM >= 70)

# Exercise 4: group_by() %>% summarize()` practice
max_lobster <- lobster_abundance %>% 
  group_by(SITE, MONTH) %>% 
  summarize(MAX_LENGTH = max(SIZE_MM, na.rm = TRUE))
```

# Exercise: Data Visualization
```{r}
# Data visualization
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(here)

lobster_abundance <- read_csv(
  "https://pasta.lternet.edu/package/data/eml/knb-lter-sbc/77/8/f32823fba432f58f66c06b589b7efac6"
) %>% 
  mutate(SIZE_MM = na_if(SIZE_MM, -99999))

# Exercise 9
ggplot(data = lobster_abundance, 
       aes(x = SIZE_MM)) +
    geom_histogram() +
    facet_wrap(~SITE)

# Exercise 10
lobsters_summarize <- lobster_abundance %>% 
  group_by(SITE, YEAR) %>% 
  summarize(COUNT = n())

# line plot
ggplot(data = lobsters_summarize, aes(x = YEAR, y = COUNT)) +
  geom_line(aes(color = SITE)) 

# line and point plot
ggplot(data = lobsters_summarize, aes(x = YEAR, y = COUNT)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = SITE)) +
  geom_line(aes(color = SITE)) 
```

```{r}
# Exercise 11
lobster_size_lrg <- lobster_abundance %>%
    filter(YEAR %in% c(2019, 2020, 2021)) %>%
    mutate(SIZE_BIN = if_else(SIZE_MM <= 70, true = "small", false = "large")) %>%
    group_by(SITE, SIZE_BIN) %>%
    summarize(COUNT = n()) %>%
    drop_na()

# bar plot
ggplot(data = lobster_size_lrg, aes(x = SITE, y = COUNT, fill = SIZE_BIN)) +
    geom_col()

# dodged bar plot
ggplot(data = lobster_size_lrg, aes(x = SITE, y = COUNT, fill = SIZE_BIN)) +
    geom_col(position = "dodge")
```

# Exercise 12: make your plots look pretty!
```{r}
# Styled dodged bar plot
lobster_size_plot <- ggplot(
  data = lobster_size_lrg, 
  aes(x = SITE, y = COUNT, fill = SIZE_BIN)
) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge", color = "black", alpha = 0.85) +
  labs(
    title = "Lobster Size Classes by Site",
    subtitle = "California spiny lobsters sampled from 2019–2021",
    x = "Site",
    y = "Number of Lobsters",
    fill = "Size Class"
  ) +
  theme_light() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    legend.position = "top"
  )

lobster_size_plot

ggsave(
  filename = here::here("figs", "lobster_size_classes_by_site.png"),
  plot = lobster_size_plot,
  width = 8,
  height = 5,
  dpi = 300
)
```
